description: This false-colour satellite image from the Canary Islands (Spain) illustrates the **wrong interpretation** for "Delimitation of water bodies using the maximum water level." The image, implicitly referencing 2012 water levels as per the surrounding context, displays a dark blue/black water body. A thick green polygon outlines this water body, representing its maximum historical extent. The surrounding land cover appears as a mosaic of red, orange, and light green patches, characteristic of agricultural fields or natural vegetation in false-colour imagery. The accompanying text labels this approach as incorrect for water body mapping in the Copernicus Land Monitoring Service (CLMS) guidelines. Credit: M. Palacios.
